patch.value: pm-cli 2026.7.11 (pm-m4iu) now stamps closed_at on close, making completed-work analytics reliable. The agent brief — our context-handoff artifact — was blind to momentum: closed items and cycle time were invisible. This adds a synthesized velocity view so an agent resuming a project immediately sees what shipped, how fast, and whether an open item is anomalously stale vs typical cycle time. Philosophy: project management = context management.
brief momentum: velocity + cycle-time context from closed_at

New '## Momentum' section in markdown/slack briefs + 'Recent momentum' in agent prompt; Standalone 'pm brief momentum' command with --days and --format text|json; Uses pm-cli 2026.7.11 closed_at (falls back to updated_at for older closes); Reports closed count, by-type, throughput/day, median+p90 cycle time, recent closes; --completed-days flag on brief/brief prompt; budget-aware trimming; all tests green

value.author: claude-opus-4.8
value.text: Implemented in index.ts: summarizeMomentum() (median + nearest-rank p90 over created_at→closed_at cycle days; itemClosedAt() prefers closed_at, falls back to updated_at). Wired momentum into buildBrief/AgentBrief; rendered in markdown, slack, and agent-prompt; added compactToBudget trimming of momentum.recent (3 then 2 under pressure). New 'brief momentum' command + --completed-days flag. 8 new unit tests (39 total pass). Functionally verified against installed pm 2026.7.11 with real closed items (markdown/json/text/window/validation).

value.author: claude-opus-4.8
value.text: Implemented summarizeMomentum() (median + nearest-rank p90 over created_at->closed_at; closed_at with updated_at fallback). Wired into buildBrief/markdown/slack/prompt + budget trimming. New 'brief momentum' command + --completed-days flag. 39 tests pass; verified against installed pm 2026.7.11 with real closed items.

value.author: claude-opus-4.8
value.text: Greptile review (468643b0) P2 addressed: itemClosedAt no longer falls back to created_at. Root fix (better than the suggested >= to >): a closed item with no closed_at/updated_at has no real close signal, so it is excluded from the window entirely rather than injecting a spurious 0-day cycle time. This preserves genuine same-day (fast-turnaround) closes as legitimate 0-day cycle data. Added regression test; 40 tests pass.

value.author: claude-opus-4.8
value.text: Gemini review round: applied all 3 findings — (1) call itemClosedAt once per item in the map; (2) byType uses Object.create(null) to avoid prototype collisions when an item type is literally 'toString'/'hasOwnProperty' (genuine accumulator bug with plain {}); (3) clamp windowDays to >=0 for the exported programmatic API. Added prototype-collision + negative-window regression tests. 42 tests pass.

value.author: claude-opus-4.8
value.text: Greptile round 2: validated all 4 prior fixes as correct, ready to merge. Applied its one observation — render throughputPerDay at its full 2-decimal precision (already rounded to 2dp in data) instead of formatScoreValue's 1dp, so rendered text matches JSON exactly and there is no silent truncation of a low-velocity metric. 42 tests pass.

patch.value: Shipped brief momentum (velocity + cycle-time from closed_at) via PR #24: markdown/slack/prompt sections, pm brief momentum command, --completed-days flag. Greptile + Gemini review clean, 42 tests pass.
